description: Acceptance bounds for live-QPU replication of the flagship 4-qubit W-state K* result.  A run that meets all three bounds is considered consistent with the shipped reference at data/w_repeat_results.json (F(K*)=0.872+/-0.021, dF=+0.330+/-0.071 across 4 runs on ibm_fez).  The bounds are set approximately 3 sigma below the reference means so a single calibration excursion does not cause a false negative.
notes: Bounds assume the shipped 137-operator K* set (data/kstar_operators_n4.json) and the W-state preparation in hardware/circuits.py.

Different backends, qubit sub-selections, or calibration epochs will produce different absolute numbers; a FAIL here means only that the run does not reproduce the flagship-quality regime, not that the protocol is broken.
To replicate the flagship reference statistics, use --backend ibm_fez --n-runs 4 --shots 1000.
f_kstar_min set ~3.4 sigma below the reference mean (0.872 - 3.4*0.021 = 0.80).

delta_f_min set conservatively at +0.10 so any decent K* advantage still passes; the reference mean is +0.33.  A failure at this level usually indicates a calibration drop or a different qubit sub-selection.
